hooks.command: bash -c 'PATH="$PWD:$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.bun/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH"; input=$(cat); file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.notebook_path // empty" 2>/dev/null); if [ -z "$file_path" ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$file_path" | grep -qE "\.syde/|\.claude/|node_modules/|vendor/|web/dist/|web/node_modules/|\.git/|^/tmp/"; then exit 0; fi; if [ ! -d .syde ]; then exit 0; fi; approved=$(syde plan list 2>/dev/null | grep -cE "approved|in-progress" || true); if [ "$approved" = "0" ]; then echo "syde BLOCK: no approved plan. Run syde plan create <name>, present it, wait for explicit chat approval, then syde plan approve <slug>. The skill is strict about this — design before code." >&2; exit 2; fi; active=$(syde task list 2>/dev/null | grep -c "●" || true); if [ "$active" = "0" ]; then echo "syde BLOCK: no active task. Start one with syde task start <slug> before writing code. Tasks track scope, files affected, and drift — writing without one breaks the audit trail." >&2; exit 2; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'PATH="$PWD:$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.bun/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH"; input=$(cat); file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // empty" 2>/dev/null); if [ -z "$file_path" ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$file_path" | grep -qE "\.syde/|\.claude/|node_modules/|vendor/|web/dist/|web/node_modules/|\.git/|^/tmp/"; then exit 0; fi; if [ ! -d .syde ]; then exit 0; fi; mapped=$(syde constraints check "$file_path" --json 2>/dev/null); if [ -z "$mapped" ] || [ "$mapped" = "{}" ]; then echo "{\"systemMessage\": \"syde: file $file_path is not mapped to any entity. Run syde update <component> --file \\\"$file_path\\\" to attach it, or it will orphan at sync check time.\"}"; fi'

bash -c 'PATH="$PWD:$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.bun/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH"; if [ ! -d .syde ]; then exit 0; fi; input=$(cat); cmd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE "git\s+commit|git\s+push"; then msgs=""; tasks=$(syde task list 2>/dev/null | grep -c "●\|○" || true); if [ "$tasks" -gt 0 ]; then msgs="$msgs $tasks tasks still pending/in_progress. Run syde task done for each."; fi; if ! syde sync check >/dev/null 2>&1; then msgs="$msgs syde sync check failed — every finding blocks (orphans, drift, stale tree, missing fields, overlap rationale gaps). Fix them all before committing."; fi; if [ -n "$msgs" ]; then echo "{\"systemMessage\": \"syde WARNING:$msgs\"}"; fi; fi'

bash -c 'PATH="$PWD:$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.bun/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH"; if [ ! -d .syde ]; then exit 0; fi; input=$(cat); cmd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" 2>/dev/null); if ! echo "$cmd" | grep -qE "syde\s+add\s+requirement"; then exit 0; fi; output=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_response.stdout // .tool_response.output // empty" 2>/dev/null); if ! echo "$output" | grep -q "overlap candidates detected"; then exit 0; fi; slugs=$(echo "$output" | grep -oE "slug: [a-z0-9-]+" | awk "{print \$2}" | tr "\n" " "); echo "{\"systemMessage\": \"syde OVERLAP RESOLUTION REQUIRED — TF-IDF surfaced candidate overlaps; text similarity is only a signal. You MUST verify semantic meaning pair-by-pair before continuing. For each overlap slug below: MERGE (drop the new requirement and reuse the existing one), RENAME (rewrite the new statement so both read distinctly, then retry), or DISTINCT (retry with --audited <slug>:\\\"why this requirement means something different in the domain\\\" for EACH slug — the rationale must be at least 20 chars and capture a real semantic difference, not a rubber stamp). Do not continue with other work until each pair is resolved. Candidate slugs: $slugs\"}"'

bash -c 'PATH="$PWD:$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.bun/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH"; if [ -d .syde ]; then ctx=$(syde context --json 2>/dev/null); tree_status=""; if ! syde tree status --strict >/dev/null 2>&1; then tree_status=" TREE SUMMARY IS STALE — run syde tree scan then iterate syde tree changes --leaves-only + syde tree summarize to clean it before planning."; fi; if [ -n "$ctx" ]; then echo "{\"systemMessage\": \"syde architecture context: $ctx$tree_status\"}"; fi; fi'

bash -c 'PATH="$PWD:$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.bun/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH"; if [ ! -d .syde ]; then exit 0; fi; syde tree scan >/dev/null 2>&1; if ! syde sync check >/dev/null 2>&1; then highlights=$(syde sync check 2>&1 | grep -E "ERROR|FINDING|Gaps:" | head -5 | tr "\n" " " | sed "s/\"/ /g"); echo "{\"systemMessage\": \"syde FINISH GATE: syde sync check failed — every finding blocks. Fix them all before ending the session (orphan files, drift, stale summary tree, missing fields, broken rels, overlap rationale gaps). Run: syde sync check. Highlights: $highlights\"}"; fi'
